messages.content:
Which Enlightenment thinker believed the rulers of a society are actually the caretakers and servants of the people in that society?
Montesquieu
Rousseau
Rousseau

During the French and Indian War, the American colonists were on the same side as which of the following? A. British
B. French
C. Native American Tribes
D. Spanish
B. French

The Stamp Act was created to do all of the following EXCEPT which one? A. Allow soldiers to live within the colonists' home.
B. Tax colonists on all paper formats.
C. Tax stamps placed on packages.
D. Force the colonists to pay more taxes.
A. Allow soldiers to live within the colonists' home.

What action of the British directly caused the Boston Tea Party? A. Coercive Acts
C. Committee of Correspondence
B. Tea Tax
D. Increased tax revenue
B. Tea Tax

What officially started on April 19, 1775? A. Boston Massacre
B. Boston Tea Party
C. Revolutionary War
D. Continental Congress
C. Revolutionary War

What was one of the effects of the Declaration of Independence?
A. The colonies formally separated from Great Britain and became the United States of America.
B. Great Britain formed an alliance with Spain against the United States.
C. King George III recognized the United States of America as a country in 1776 and withdrew his army.
A. The colonies formally separated from Great Britain and became the United States of America.

Who was assigned the duty of actually writing the Declaration of Independence? A. Thomas Jefferson
B. Richard Henry Lee
C. Ben Franklin
D. John Adams
A. Thomas Jefferson

According to the passage, what section of the Declaration of Independence outlines
the responsibility of a government?
A. list of grievances
B. preamble
C. resolution of independence
D. declaration of rights
D. declaration of rights

Which of the following was NOT a power given to the Central Government by the Articles of Confederation? A. build an army
C. collect taxes
B. receive and send ambassadors
D. sign treaties
C. collect taxes

After the passage of the Articles of
Confederation, why were states left to enforce the
law?
A. The federal government was too busy dealing with war and peace issues related to foreign nations.
B. There was confusion as to which laws to enforce.
C. The states and articles did not grant the federal government the power to enforce domestic laws.
D. After dealing with a King, early colonists wanted to be free from any enforcement of the law.
C. The states and articles did not grant the federal government the power to enforce domestic laws.

After the creation of the Articles of
Confederation, which of the following was true
about state
governments?
A. States had the power to negotiate treaties with foreign countries as individual states.
B. States were able to collect taxes following the Revolutionary War.
C. States were not able to collect taxes from American citizens following the Revolutionary War.
A. States had the power to negotiate treaties with foreign countries as individual states.

In which state was Shays' Rebellion? A. Pennsylvania
B. New York
C. Virginia
D. Massachusetts
D. Massachusetts

Which of the following created the House of
Representatives and the
Senate as we know them
today?
A. the New Jersey Plan
B. the Virginia Plan
C. the Connecticut Compromise
C. the Connecticut Compromise

The "Federalists and Anti- Federalists" groups were the beginning of what movement? A. The Enlightenment Age
B. Rise of Democracy
C. American Political Parties
C. American Political Parties
